verdict.summary: A good business at a fair price — not a great business at a great price. Starter position only at today's quote; back up the truck on AV-fear sell-offs.

pros: Asset-light marketplace converting **$10B+ FCF** on $54B revenue (18.8% FCF margin).[^uber-10k-2024]
Network-effect moat: 2–3× Lyft share in US, #1/2 in most of 70+ countries.[^uber-investor-day-2024]
Embedded high-margin **advertising business** (~$1.5B run-rate) compounding fastest.[^uber-q4-2024-pr]
Capital return inflection: **$6.5B buyback in 2025** after first-ever in 2024.[^uber-q4-2024-pr]
ROIC 19% vs WACC 10% — real economic profit.
cons: **Autonomous vehicles** — Tesla/Waymo could bypass the demand layer. Genuine unknown.[^waymo-milestone-2025]
**Driver classification** regulation risk (PRO Act, EU PWD enforcement).[^ab5-prop22]
SBC of **$1.86B/yr** dilutes real shareholder yield from 5% to ~3%.[^uber-10k-2024]
Insiders own only **0.17%**; no founder remains on the cap table.[^uber-def14a-2025]
Insurance accruals are actuarial and lumpy — CA verdicts can swing margin 200bp.[^uber-10k-2024]

sections.label: Section 1 of 3
sections.title: Business · Do I truly understand it?

questions.title: What does Uber actually do?
questions.body: Uber runs an app that matches three groups who have something to give with three groups who need it: **riders ↔ drivers**, **eaters ↔ restaurants ↔ couriers**, **shippers ↔ truckers**. It owns the matching engine — maps, pricing, ratings, fraud checks — and skims ~25–30¢ of every dollar that flows through.[^uber-10k-2024]

questions.title: How do they make money, and from whom?
questions.sub: Who pays, for what, at what margin.
questions.footnote: Hidden engine: **advertising** (~$1.5B run-rate, very high incremental margin) — most under-appreciated lever in the P&L.[^uber-q4-2024-pr]

questions.title: Am I pretending to understand it, or do I really?

left: Why Mobility > Delivery as a business (denser network, less SKUs)
Why asset-light platforms compound FCF post-scale
right: Gross-vs-net revenue accounting by jurisdiction (real complexity)
True unit economics in an AV world (driver line gone, but exclusivity over supply gone too)

questions.title: Happy owning it 10 years if the market shut tomorrow?
questions.body: Yes — provided management doesn't burn $10B trying to "own AVs too," driver-classification rules don't get globally codified into employment, and buybacks continue meaningfully shrinking share count. The 10-year window is exactly when the AV question resolves; Uber is positioned on _both_ sides as partner and demand aggregator.

questions.title: What could kill this business?
riskMatrix: Consumer-app switching cost; Uber's demand-pattern data
Delivery now profitable, not a cash sink

sections.label: Section 2 of 3
sections.title: Management · Can I trust these people with my capital?

questions.title: Trust them to run my family's money?
questions.body: **Dara Khosrowshahi** (CEO since Aug 2017) inherited a dumpster fire from Travis Kalanick. Eight years later: GAAP profitable, S&P 500 (Dec 2023)[^sp500-add-2023], $10B FCF, no major scandals. From Expedia, where he was a respected low-drama operator (2005–2017).[^khosrowshahi-bio-2017] New CFO **Prashanth Mahendra-Rajah** (2024) comes from Analog Devices — manufacturing-discipline DNA, fitting Uber's evolution from growth-at-all-costs to compound-cash.

left: Killed dual-class super-vote in 2023 — one share, one vote[^uber-def14a-2025]
Segment-level economics disclosed in plain English since 2022
right: Foodpanda Taiwan deal blocked by regulators Dec 2024 — still an M&A itch[^foodpanda-taiwan-2024]

timeline.note: Burned ~$10B on China, SE Asia, ATG. Disaster, but instructive.
Sold China→Didi, SE Asia→Grab, Russia→Yandex, ATG→Aurora[^aurora-atg-2020] — for equity. Bought Postmates ($2.65B), Cornershop. **Excellent.**
May-22 "sea change" memo: cost discipline, meritocracy at promotion.[^dara-sea-change-2022] Stopped speculative M&A. **Strong.**
Initiated buybacks, paid down converts, Foodpanda Taiwan blocked.[^foodpanda-taiwan-2024] **Good so far, watch closely.**

questions.title: Do they tell the truth in good years and bad?
bullets: **FY22 (bad):** $9.1B GAAP loss from equity write-downs — Dara owned it, didn't hide behind non-GAAP.[^uber-10k-2022]
**FY23 (breakthrough):** Did _not_ spike the football; warned 2023 GAAP NI was flattered by equity revaluations and wouldn't repeat.[^uber-10k-2023]
**FY24:** Led with the disclosure that $5.8B of NI came from a deferred-tax valuation allowance release — a one-time non-cash gain. Could have buried it. Didn't.[^uber-10k-2024][^uber-letter-2024]

questions.title: Real skin in the game?
questions.body: Total insider ownership **0.17%** (~$258M).[^uber-def14a-2025] Dara holds ~1.5M shares + options (~$110–150M) — meaningful for him but he sells programmatically every quarter via 10b5-1. **Travis Kalanick exited his entire 8.6% stake by 2020.**[^kalanick-exit-2019] No founder remains. Compare Meta (Zuckerberg 13%), Amazon (Bezos 9%). This is firmly the "professionally-managed" camp — incentives run through comp plan design, not equity ownership. The 2026 proxy is worth reading: TSR-linked PSUs = positive signal; pure RSU grants = neutral-negative.

sections.label: Section 3 of 3
sections.title: Price · Am I paying a sensible price?

questions.title: What am I paying today?
questions.sub: P/E, earnings yield, or whichever lens fits.
questions.body: Most honest single number: **16× EV/FCF** on a business that compounded FCF ~25% over four years. Not expensive. Not the bargain it was at $40 in 2022. **Fair.**

questions.title: What is the market implicitly expecting?
questions.body: Reverse-DCF: today's price requires ~10–12% FCF CAGR for 10 years, then 3% terminal. My base case: 15–18% near-term, 10–12% mid-term FCF growth as Delivery scales and advertising mixes up. Conclusion: **the market is pricing roughly what I'd model — no big upside or downside embedded.** The 52-week -18.6% reflects fears about Tesla robotaxi (Austin) and Waymo (SF/LA), in my view over-discounted: Waymo today ~250k rides/wk[^waymo-milestone-2025] vs Uber ~33M/day globally.[^uber-q4-2024-pr]

questions.title: What would I pay for the whole business?
questions.footnote: Today's quote ≈ what a rational private buyer would pay. Nibble below **$65** (13% MOS, ~13× EV/FCF). Back up the truck below **$55** (~11× EV/FCF on a 15%-grower).

questions.title: At what price would I sell, and why?

triggers.title: Fundamental trigger
triggers.body: FCF margin contracts 2 consecutive Qs with no one-off cause → AV take-rate compression is real → exit.

triggers.title: Capital allocation trigger
triggers.body: > $10B acquisition or re-entry into China / own AV fleet → exit.

questions.title: If it drops 40% next month — buy or panic?
questions.body: $45 ≈ market cap $92B, EV/FCF ~9×. **Pre-commitment: buy aggressively,** unless the drop is accompanied by one of:
ordered: Federal US or top-3 market driver-reclassification ruling
Step-function AV consumer adoption (Tesla 100k+ public robotaxis or Waymo signing a Tesla-style OEM deal + direct apps in 10+ cities)
Dara departure / large insider sale / surprise CFO change
questions.footnote: The pre-commitment matters more than the analysis. Most investors fail not by picking wrong, but by selling at $45 and buying back at $90.

scorecard.summary: Position sizing: 2–3% at today's quote; scale to 5–6% on AV-fear sell-off. Conviction: Medium-High on business, Medium on management, Medium on price.

sources.note: $9.1B GAAP loss; equity-investment write-downs disclosed transparently.

sources.title: Dara Khosrowshahi — 2024 Letter to Shareholders
sources.note: Tone-and-honesty assessment: led with deferred-tax allowance disclosure rather than burying it.

sources.title: Uber Technologies — 2025 Proxy Statement (DEF 14A)
sources.note: Insider ownership %, executive comp design (TSR PSUs vs RSUs), board composition.

sources.title: Uber 2024 Investor Day — Long-term financial framework
sources.note: Long-term outlook for FCF conversion, Mobility/Delivery EBITDA targets, geographic share data.

sources.title: Uber CEO tells staff company will cut down on hiring, treat it as a 'privilege' — 'sea change' memo

sources.title: Aurora to acquire Uber ATG self-driving unit; Uber takes Aurora equity stake
sources.note: Capital-allocation milestone: Uber exited the most expensive ATG investment cycle for equity rather than cash.

sources.title: Taiwan's FTC blocks Uber's $950M acquisition of Foodpanda Taiwan
sources.note: Regulatory block signal — capital-allocation 'M&A itch' yellow flag for the Dara P3 era.

sources.title: Waymo One surpasses 250,000 paid rides per week across PHX, SF, LA
sources.note: Useful for the AV-pacing argument: Waymo at ~250k/week vs Uber's ~33M/day globally — orders-of-magnitude gap.

sources.title: Travis Kalanick sells nearly all his Uber stake post-lockup; departs board end-2019
sources.note: Founder exit confirmation — establishes the 'no founder remains' status for Q2.5.
